chief_complaint: A 65-year-old right-handed male patient presented to the emergency department with a few hours' history of mild confusion and difficulty expressing himself verbally.
history: The patient has a history of hypertension and cured hairy cell leukemia. He presented to the emergency department with a few hours' history of mild confusion and difficulty expressing himself verbally.
exam: Vital signs: BP 140/90 mmHg, HR 80 bpm. Neurologic exam revealed the patient could not read at all, but he could write perfectly with preserved spelling and handwriting.
labs: CT scan of the brain showed a 5 cm infarction in the left occipital lobe, and a corresponding P2 occlusion was seen. MRI of the brain showed infarctions corresponding with prior case reports on the diagnosis.
